title: Edmonton Oilers travel to Dallas for Game 1 of the WCF
summary: It’s Game 1 of the Western Conference Final, and the Edmonton Oilers are back in familiar territory. On today’s edition of Oilersnation Everyday, we get you fully prepped for the series opener between the Oilers and the Dallas Stars — a rematch of last year’s WCF, which saw Edmonton advance in six games. This year, the Oilers enter the third round looking like a more complete, battle-tested team. Their depth has stepped up, their blue line is more reliable, and they’re playing their best hockey at the right time. Meanwhile, the Dallas Stars continue to be one of the NHL’s most consistent contenders, reaching the Western Final for the third straight season and bringing back much of the core that has made them so tough to beat. We’ll break down all the key storylines leading into Game 1, including lineup updates for both teams, possible changes on the Oilers' side, and how the Stars may try to match up with Edmonton’s top six. We'll deliver our full Game 1 lineup report, go through key game notes, share our one big take, ask the Sherwood Ford Giant Question, and fire up the gameday Bet365 betting challenge. Plus, we’ll be joined by former Edmonton Oilers defenseman Ladi Smid to dive into the WCF repeat matchup. We'll get his thoughts on both teams top lines, as well as their depth down the lineup. How much will this matchup determine the series? And who has the edge heading into Game 1? All that and everything else you need to know before puck drop, right here on Oilersnation Everyday! title: Marcellus Wiley TALKS Ryan Clark & RGIII Beef | Angel Reese Is the 'Great Black Hoax' | Ep 932
summary: Angel Reese is back in the center ring, and this time Jason is calling the spectacle exactly what it is: “The Great Black Hoax.” In today’s episode of "Fearless," the fireworks are just getting started as Marcellus Wiley joins the discussion with his trademark blunt-force truth. Whitlock rolls the tape on Monday night’s sideline mystery — Reese ducking every postgame question, assistant coach Tyler Marsh claiming total amnesia, and a sudden TikTok tear-jerker meant to crown Reese as the latest martyr of “protected class” politics. Jason breaks down the receipts and asks the question ESPN won’t touch: Is America about to fall for another Jussie-style hustle dressed up as women’s empowerment? Nothing is off-limits: Ryan Clark’s social media simping and race-baiting feud with RG3, Bill Belichick’s headline-grabbing May-December romance with Jordon Hudson, Joy Taylor’s flirty flirtation with X-rated clicks, and Stephen A. Smith’s delusional flirtation with the presidency. Whitlock and Wiley shred the sacred cows, torch the grift, and leave no blue check unscathed. Jason dissects Caitlin Clark’s shocking loss: a coach asleep at the wheel, veterans DeWanna Bonner and Natasha Howard clanking down the stretch, and a league that seems more interested in humiliation for clicks than basketball excellence. Is the WNBA sabotaging its own golden goose? Whitlock connects the dots and exposes the agenda. It’s raw, it’s fearless, and it’s the smartest sports talk on the internet. title: Sukh Sadhra [#54]: Selling Out the Premiership Rugby Final Two Years Running
podcast_name: Sports Pundit Podcast with Andy Marston
summary: On today’s episode, I’m joined by Sukh Sadhra, Head of Marketing at Premiership Rugby - the top tier of English club rugby union and home to some of the most iconic teams in the game. Sukh’s background spans roles at Betfair, Sky, and experience-tech company Pollen, where he led CRM and brand communications, giving him a uniquely data-driven and fan-first approach to sports marketing. In this conversation, we unpack Sukh’s marketing philosophy, how he’s repositioning Premiership Rugby for a new generation of fans, and why the Final at Twickenham has become a sell-out event two years running. We talk creativity, conversion, culture, and what it takes to turn a rugby match into an unmissable experience. Timestamps 02:00 - Sukh’s Journey 08:00 - Data-Driven Marketing Strategies 18:00 - Collaboration with Clubs and Centralized Data 24:00 - Joint Marketing Efforts with RFU 28:00 - Building Player Profiles and Social Media Presence 32:00 - Selling the Final as a Premier Event 41:00 - Influencer Partnerships and Social Media 48:00 - Future Focus: Rebranding and Player Strategy Additional Links The Gallagher Premiership Final is officially SOLD-OUT! title: The Edmonton Oilers prepare for the WCF + Louie DeBrusk
summary: The Western Conference Final is officially set, and the Edmonton Oilers are just four wins away from a trip to the Stanley Cup Final. On today’s edition of Oilersnation Everyday, we preview the upcoming series between the Oilers and the Dallas Stars, which is set to begin Wednesday night in Texas. The Oilers are riding high after dispatching the Vegas Golden Knights in five games, while the Stars are coming off a tough six-game battle with the Winnipeg Jets. We'll continue to dive into the biggest storylines heading into this highly anticipated series, including the possible return of Mattias Ekholm to the Oilers' lineup, potential line combination changes from Kris Knoblauch, and whether the depth can continue to shine. We'll also look at the other side, where Roope Hintz and Mikko Rantanen have powered the Stars' offense and a stout Dallas blueline led by Miro Heiskanen will present a new challenge for Connor McDavid and company. We'll break it all down with our usual features: our lineup report, Sherwood Ford Giant Question, one big take, and our playoff report — which also includes a look at the Eastern Conference Final between the Florida Panthers and Carolina Hurricanes, set to begin tonight. Joining us on the show is Sportsnet commentator Louie DeBrusk, who will give his insight on the series and share what he thinks will be key to victory for both clubs. Don't be surprised if former NHL goalie Devan Dubnyk drops in to offer a goaltender's perspective on the matchup between Jake Oettinger and whoever gets the nod for Edmonton. All that and more coming your way on this playoff edition of Oilersnation Everyday! title: Ryan Clark SIMPS for Angel Reese, Calls Out RGIII for Marrying White Woman | Ep 931
summary: Angel Reese is Jussie Smollett 2.0. Have we learned nothing? On today’s episode of "Fearless," Jason tackles the media storm surrounding Angel Reese’s latest play for racial victimhood after a blowout loss to Caitlin Clark and the Indiana Fever on WNBA opening day. Despite her poor performance and emotional meltdown, Reese is once again being defended by the usual suspects — Ryan Clark, Emmanuel Acho, and others desperate to reframe criticism as racism. Meanwhile, more grounded voices like Robert Griffin III are calling it for what it is: personal hatred masquerading as activism. Jason breaks down the receipts — from Reese’s taunts in the NCAA Championship to her ongoing obsession with belittling Clark. Caitlin Clark made Reese famous, and ever since, Reese has been determined to rewrite the story as one of oppression instead of obsession. But that’s only the tip of the iceberg. The show also dives into the escalating tension between media personalities Ryan Clark and RG3. Is this really about the WNBA, or is there something deeper brewing between the two? Jason and Steve Kim analyze the underlying dynamics — identity, ideology, and even family life. Does RG3’s interracial marriage play a role in his worldview? Why does Ryan Clark bend the knee so quickly to black female idolatry? Later in the show, Shemeka Michelle joins the conversation and offers her take on the deeper motivations behind the Clark-Reese dynamic and the Clark-Griffin debate. This episode is packed with the truth bombs mainstream media won’t touch. Don’t miss a minute — only on "Fearless." ​​Today's Sponsors: PreBorn PreBorn empowers women facing unplanned pregnancies to choose life by offering free ultrasounds and compassionate support. title: Oilers-Stars WCF preview + Maple Leafs humiliated
summary: It’s a special May Long Weekend edition of Oilersnation Everyday! While it’s a shorter episode, there’s still a ton to get into after a wild weekend of Stanley Cup Playoffs action. The Western Conference Final is officially set, as the Dallas Stars eliminated the Winnipeg Jets in six games to book a rematch with the Edmonton Oilers. We’ll dive into the key takeaways from that series, look at how both teams are currently constructed, and assess how the Oilers stack up against a deep, structured Stars squad. Are the Oilers better equipped to handle Dallas this year? Over in the East, Game 7 between the Toronto Maple Leafs and Florida Panthers turned into an absolute blowout. The Panthers steamrolled Toronto with a 6-1 win, ending another disappointing playoff run for the Leafs and advancing to their third straight Conference Final. We’ll talk about what went so wrong for Toronto and just how scary this Panthers team looks heading into the next round. No guests today, just a good ol’ fashioned holiday show with playoff takes, analysis, and some fun ahead of the Oilers’ next big test. title: Angel Reese Embodies Black Fatigue | Stephen A Smith: The Next Joe Biden or Joe Rogan | Ep 930
summary: The WNBA is back — and Angel Reese is already making headlines for all the wrong reasons. On a much-anticipated opening night, the Chicago Sky traveled to Indianapolis to face Caitlin Clark and the Indiana Fever. The game did not disappoint. Clark posted her first triple-double of the season, while Angel Reese completely lost her composure in an on-court altercation. Jason breaks down the WNBA's weak attempt to excuse Reese’s behavior — once again blaming racism instead of holding her accountable. Is the narrative around Angel Reese finally falling apart? Later in the show, Steve Kim joins the conversation to weigh in on Reese, along with Joy Taylor’s odd endorsement of prostitution, the latest in the Aaron Rodgers-Steelers signing buzz, and Bill Belichick’s baffling explanation for why his 20-something “sugar baby” Jordon Hudson plays such a big role in his life. Colin Cowherd jumps into the Belichick conversation, suggesting Jordon’s main concern is what’s in the will. Cam Newton also shares some “relationship advice,” but should someone with many kids by multiple women be giving anyone relationship guidance? To close the show, Jason revisits Stephen A.  Smith’s phantom presidential aspirations. A New York Times article reveals what might be his real plan: to become the Joe Rogan of the Democratic Party. How would the “face of ESPN” pull that off? And in breaking news, President Joe Biden reveals he has an aggressive form of prostate cancer. Were media outlets covering this up the whole time? After the show, hop over to the Jason Whitlock Harmony channel, as Jason and Shemeka Michelle react to the Chicago mayor's controversial comments about hiring only black people. title: Lucy Trang Nguyen [Innovation #01] : Future of XR in Sports and Entertainment
podcast_name: Sports Pundit Podcast with Andy Marston
summary: This is the first episode in a series of 5 focused on #Innovation and hosted by Leanne Bats, a former All Blacks and FC Barcelona executive now leading innovation for a regional sports trust in New Zealand.  On today’s episode of the Sports Pundit Innovation Podcast, I’m joined by Lucy Trang Nguyen, Business Development Director for Emerging Technologies at Accedo, and one of the key voices shaping the future of immersive sports experiences. Lucy has been leading the launch of the XR Sports Alliance, a powerful new collective founded by Accedo, Qualcomm and HBS, working to bring extended reality - or XR - from proof-of-concept into real-world fan experiences. Lucy and her team are helping sports organisations rethink what’s possible when fans aren’t limited by a 2D screen. In this conversation, we unpack the promise of XR - what it actually is, how it could change both the at-home and in-venue experience, and why rights holders, broadcasters, and sponsors should start paying close attention. We also explore what makes XR storytelling different, the commercial and legal challenges of immersive content, and what needs to happen next to make it scalable. Timestamps 02:00 Lucy's Career Journey and Early AR Experiences 10:00 The XR Sports Alliance: Formation and Mission 14:00 The Current State and Future of XR in Sports 27:00 In-Venue Experiences and the Potential of XR 30:00 Streaming Innovations for Euro 2024 34:00 Modular Viewing Experiences and Sponsorship Integration 38:00 Challenges and Learnings in XR Sports Alliance 50:00 Future of XR in Sports and Entertainment Additional Links Connect with Lucy on LinkedIn - Here Connect with Leanne on LinkedIn - Here

title: Could Mattias Ekholm return for the Edmonton Oilers? + Jason Demers
summary: The Edmonton Oilers are officially halfway to the ultimate goal. With two playoff rounds down and two still to go, it’s the perfect time to pause and reflect on what’s been an unforgettable postseason journey so far. From early stumbles to dominant performances, emotional highs and gut-wrenching lows, the Oilers have ridden the full rollercoaster—but have come out of it looking like a team hitting their stride at exactly the right time. On today’s episode of Oilersnation Everyday, we’ll take a step back and evaluate everything that’s brought the Oilers to this point. How has their game evolved since the puck dropped on Round 1? What’s been the biggest difference in their structure, and where have the key turning points been? We’ll also look ahead to Round 3, discussing potential lineup combinations, the injury status of Mattias Ekholm, and the looming goaltending decision between Calvin Pickard and Stuart Skinner. We’ll deliver our One Big Take, ask the Sherwood Ford Giant Question, and go around the rest of the league with our NHL Playoff Report as the Final Four begins to take shape. To wrap things up, we’ll serve up some weekend picks with help from our friends at Bet365. The Oilers are heating up—and if the second half of the playoffs is anything like the first, we’re in for a wild ride. title: The Edmonton Oilers are headed back to the Western Conference Finals
summary: The Edmonton Oilers are heading back to the Western Conference Final—again. It only took five games to dispatch the Vegas Golden Knights, as the Oilers shut out them out 1-0 in an intense, defensive Game 5 showdown. It was playoff hockey at its tightest, with both teams playing a conservative, high-pressure game from puck drop to the final buzzer. But it was the Oilers who found the breakthrough. After 60 minutes of scoreless hockey, the game headed to overtime where Kasperi Kapanen played the hero. The veteran winger crashed the crease and banged home a loose puck at the side of the net, giving the Oilers the win and officially punching their ticket to the Western Conference Final for the third time in the last four seasons. It was another masterclass from Stuart Skinner, who recorded his second straight shutout—stopping all 24 shots he faced. He now sits at 127 consecutive minutes of shutout hockey and is peaking at the right time. On today’s episode of Oilersnation Everyday, we’ll break down everything from Game 5. From our full scoring summary to the biggest moments of the night, we’ll take you through how the Oilers got it done. We’ll give you our One Big Take, tackle the Sherwood Ford Giant Question, and go around the rest of the NHL as the second round winds down and we wait to see who Edmonton’s next opponent will be. The job isn’t finished, but the Oilers have made a major statement—and the city of Edmonton is buzzing. Let’s talk about it. title: Cork v Limerick - the Sliding Doors rivalry that’s all drama
summary: As red and green are primed to collide again, Fintan O’Toole, Declan Bogue and Ronan Early chart the small moments that have had big consequences in this duel over the past nine years. Limerick stole a march on the Rebels in 2017 and 2018 and reigned supreme until 2024, when Cork rose again. Can Limerick put the red genie back in the bottle? Can Cork drive home their advantage? We discuss another hotly anticipated clash between the southern powerhouses. Also, can Dublin’s footballers answer their critics? The majesty of the Ulster and Leinster finals - and some ugly scenes around these otherwise life-affirming occasions.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Leanne Bats [#53]: Taking Lessons from FC Barcelona and the All Blacks to a Regional Sports Trust
podcast_name: Sports Pundit Podcast with Andy Marston
summary: On today’s episode I’m joined by Leanne Bats, Head of Digital and Innovation at Sport Waikato, a not for profit Regional Sports Trust in New Zealand with the vision of 'Everyone out there and active'.  You might be thinking it’s fairly unique for a regional sports trust to have someone leading innovation, and you’d be right. This isn’t a typical role, it’s one that has been created specifically for Leanne - a former Football Fern, who has gone on to work with some of the biggest sporting IP on the planet in the form of the FC Barcelona and the All Blacks. In this conversation, we discuss how she’s implementing innovation to increase participation in Waikato, a region in the upper North Island of New Zealand. We also discuss what she has learnt from her time with NZ rugby, her philosophy towards innovation, as well as who inspires her. Timestamps 3:00 - Leanne’s Journey from the Football Ferns to the All Blacks 9:00 - Participation as a Form of Fan Engagement 15:00 - Engaging Younger Generations - and Meeting them Where They Are 21:00 - Implementing Innovation 29:00 - Innovators Who Inspire Leanne 37:00 - Future Plans and a Big Announcement Additional Links Connect with Leanne on LinkedIn - Here Connect with Andy on LinkedIn - Here

title: Glen Boss - Kevie Walters Inside Ball
summary: This week on Kevie Walters Inside Ball, Kevie is joined by champion Aussie jockey and all-round legend, Glen Boss.  The two dive into Glen’s incredible journey — from his early days growing up, to his iconic racing career, including that unforgettable partnership with Makybe Diva. Glen also opens up about one of the scariest moments of his life: the fall that left him with a broken neck.  Plus, a few classic yarns from nights out with Kevie, Alfie and the boys that you won’t want to miss.  Another cracking episode with one of Australia’s sporting greats. title: Peak jeopardy for Clare and Tipp | McGeeney deep dive
summary: The Munster hurling championship is all about peril, and we have a stark case of that on Saturday night when Clare and Tipperary face off in Ennis. A loss for either side will likely mean the end for their 2025 campaign - on May 10. As Jackie Tyrrell said, careers and seasons are on the line here. On today’s pod, Fintan O’Toole, Declan Bogue and Ronan Early get into the peril that faces both sets of players and management teams. They also ponder the fact that most of the country won’t get to see this do-or-die clash, with it not being on free-to-air television. In the Ulster championship, Donegal face Kieran McGeeney’s Armagh. Today we put a sharp focus on the All-Ireland champion’s long-serving manager. We try to get to the nub of how he has been able to inspire such extraordinary levels of loyalty from players, in the good times and the hard.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: MNAsplaining: Kayleigh meets Ruesha Littlejohn
summary: This week, Kayleigh is joined in the studio by none other than Irish footballing icon Ruesha Littlejohn. With an impressive 88 caps for Ireland and a celebrated career in the UK, the talented midfielder has now brought her skills to the Women’s League of Ireland, signing with Shamrock Rovers. In this lively episode, Ruesha shares her earliest footballing memories, reflects on the highs and lows of her journey, and even takes part in a surprise karaoke showdown with Kayleigh.  The conversation also delves into life on both sides of the Irish Sea, as Ruesha opens up about her experiences in Ireland and Scotland, comparing the culture, the craic, and, of course, the all-important food and drink. It’s an episode packed with laughs, insights, and a few unexpected moments you won’t want to miss. talkSPORT Ireland - MNÁsplaining with Kayleigh Trappe  Hosted on Acast.
